rag_rules: Insert AGREE annex blocks into component implementations when binding implementation-level inputs or outputs, and into component types for visible feature contracts.
generic_rules: Preserve AADL scope boundaries: type-level contracts must use only visible ports; implementation-level contracts may bind subcomponent ports via valid scope.
Ensure AGREE scalar types are declared as int, real, or bool—never use AADL-type aliases like integer or boolean.
syntax_or_pattern_reminders: Use AGREE logical operators: and, or, not(...), =>; avoid Boolean arithmetic or numeric conversions for Boolean ports.
Declare constants and equations with explicit types matching their expressions: eq result : bool = x >= min_value;
anti_patterns: Do not reference subcomponent ports in component type annexes—only use visible features defined in the type interface.
Do not convert Boolean ports to numeric comparisons unless the AADL data definition explicitly supports scalar range semantics.
